narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A core API platform whose latency rises simultaneously across all three tiers during peak hours, with no clear lead-lag relationship. CPU and connection counts are within normal ranges on all tiers — the problem is latency-distributed, not capacity-driven. Root cause is ambiguous from the observable signals alone.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the system's ability to recognize when *not* to recommend aggressive action. The data shows a problem, but the root cause could be a downstream provider, a shared queue, an external dependency, or coordination overhead — none observable from this telemetry. Recommending scaling without diagnosis risks worsening the problem.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Recommend full end-to-end distributed trace analysis to identify the actual root cause before making any scaling or rightsizing decisions. Aggressive action without diagnosis is contraindicated.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation defers to trace analysis. A great recommendation explicitly enumerates the candidate root causes (downstream provider, shared queue, external dependency, coordination overhead) and notes that the current telemetry is insufficient to choose between them.

before_after_evidence.config_before: 10 m5.large + db.r6g.xlarge + basic VPC peering (no optimization across tiers)
before_after_evidence.config_after: End-to-end distributed trace analysis identified actual root cause (varies by environment); targeted intervention applied
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: Distributed tracing surfaced the actual root cause in each case (downstream provider, shared queue, or external dependency). Targeted intervention reduced latency significantly while SLA 99.9% stayed preserved throughout the diagnostic window.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across core-API-platform cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Recommend full end-to-end distributed trace analysis to identify the actual root cause (candidates include downstream provider latency, shared queue contention, external dependency timeouts, or coordination overhead) before making any scaling or rightsizing decisions. The current telemetry is insufficient to choose between these candidates. Aggressive action without diagnosis risks worsening the problem.
